title: Pronounciation of Evangelion That Is Not How You Say It
display_title: Pronounciation of Evangelion That Is Not How You Say It
wikitext:
[]: Pronounciation of Evangelion: That Is (Not) How You Say It             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Oct 21, 2013 6:45 am It seems even the way to say the names and phrases in the series can cause mass debates and rampant anger (One Hour Photo anyone?). So, basically, this is the thread for discussing mispronounciations of names and words in Evangelion that you or others have made. Myself, for a long, long time I was (I am ashamed to admit) pronouncing Evangelion as "Ee-van-jee-lee-on", which is possibly even worse than Evanjellyon. What can I say, I was an idiot. Anyway, your tales of pronounciation blunders?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Oct 21, 2013 7:37 am I avoid physical Eva gatherings like the plague. I went to one and everyone was saying Kow aru instead of Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:03 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: everyone was saying Kaworu instead of Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:05 am We have a word filter for people who say Kow aru.

[]: Re: Pronounciation of Evangelion: That Is (Not) How You Say             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:43 am Squigsquasher wrote: Myself, for a long, long time I was (I am ashamed to admit) pronouncing Evangelion as "Ee-van-jee-lee-on", which is possibly even worse than Evanjellyon. What can I say, I was an idiot.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 21, 2013 9:12 am Listening to the Japanese helps, as does knowing that Japanese vowels are often swallowed (to help register the sound against the subtitles).

[]:             Quote Post by ShockTrance » Mon Oct 21, 2013 11:08 am I used to pronounce Evangelion with a soft G before I watched the series, but that stopped as soon as I heard the characters say it. A friend of mine who's seen Eva pronounces Asuka as "Uh-Soo-Kuh", which is strange, considering that he watched the sub and thus heard the correct pronunciation. TMBounty_Hunter wrote: I went to one and everyone was saying Kow aru instead of Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 21, 2013 11:30 am could be worse... they might have been saying "Kwaaaruu"

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Mon Oct 21, 2013 11:32 am NemZ wrote: could be worse... they might have been saying "Kwaaaruu"

[]: Re: Pronounciation of Evangelion: That Is (Not) How You Say             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 21, 2013 11:40 am I still struggle to pronounce "Eva" and "Evangelion" long after I learned the correct pronunciation. I've gotten better with it after I made a conscious effort to try and change it last year but I still catch myself saying "Eeva" or "Eevangelion" from time to time. Also Seele which I want to pronounce seal. And for that matter maybe Wille since I don't know how the fuck that's pronounced. I should probably load up 3.0 and see... Bagheera wrote: I'm still getting out of the habit of pronouncing the U in Asuka's name. It's helped to think of her name as a feminized version of Oscar.

[]: Re: Pronounciation of Evangelion: That Is (Not) How You Say             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 21, 2013 12:51 pm CJD wrote: And for that matter maybe Wille since I don't know how the fuck that's pronounced.

[]:             Quote Post by tzigi » Mon Oct 21, 2013 12:53 pm You know what the fun thing is? That Evangelion should be pronounced "Ewanghelion" [gh] standing for hard g (think "spaGHetti"). That's the proper Ancient Greek pronounciation of its source word.

[]: Re: Pronounciation of Evangelion: That Is (Not) How You Say             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 21, 2013 1:01 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Try the little speaker icon at http://translate.google.com/#auto/en/Wille

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Oct 21, 2013 2:03 pm If you're used to reading German, that's exactly what it looks like!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:29 pm How come I see so many Eva-related vids on YT with people who claim to know the show saying 'Rit-Sue-Ko' and 'A-Suh-Ka'. That drives me round the bend.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:45 pm Jayfive wrote: How come I see so many Eva-related vids on YT with people who claim to know the show saying 'Rit-Sue-Ko' and 'A-Suh-Ka'. That drives me round the bend.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Oct 21, 2013 8:52 pm Let's ask the S2 Works to remind us how to say it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 21, 2013 9:45 pm Bagheera wrote: If Eva can use a nonsensical pronunciation of Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Joy Evangelion » Mon Oct 21, 2013 9:46 pm Before I watched the series I bought my copy of the platinum edition at this store that sells used games and dvds and etc. that they keep stored in this locked window case, so if you want to buy something you've got to tell the staff to unlock the case and get it for you. So, I told the dude working there that I wanted the "evanjellion" dvds, and I still feel like a dick about it. I'll probably never get over it... Oh, I also dislike how the English cast pronounces Nerv as "Nerve," but I get a huge kick out of the way the narrator on the Manga Entertainment EoE trailer pronounces Seele. He should stick to quoting Milton

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Oct 21, 2013 10:32 pm I think I pronounce everything right. I used to pronounce Asuka Ah-su-ka until I saw the show. Ritsuko will always be Ritt-sue-kho to me no matter what. I can't help it, my brain refuses to parse that word differently.
